title: Bulbophyllum pahudii
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum pahudii is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the phylum Angiospermophyta, and further categorized as belonging to the class Liliopsida. It is also classified under the order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes many species of orchids. Within this order, Bulbophyllum pahudii falls under the family Orchidaceae. The genus Bulbophyllum is a large and diverse group of orchids that includes over 2,000 species, and Bulbophyllum pahudii is one of the species within this genus. Its classification provides a framework for understanding the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.

intro: Bulbophyllum pahudii, also known as Pahud's Bulbophyllum, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its unique, bulbous structure and vibrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum pahudii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies for various health conditions.
